articles.title: Mamata Hits Streets In Kolkata, Threatens TMC Will Protest In Delhi If Blocked

articles.title: 'Sonia Gandhi had faith in me, Rahul a constant source of guidance': CM-designate DK Shivakumar on his political journey
articles.description: Karnataka CM-designate D K Shivakumar thanked Sonia, Rahul, Rajiv and Priyanka Gandhi for shaping his political journey. Ahead of his June 3 swearing-in, he said their trust, guidance and support helped him rise from grassroots worker to Karnataka leader.

articles.title: SK Hynix plans to double capacity to ease memory chip crunch
articles.description: SK Hynix will do whatever it requires to fund that expansion in chip wafer capacity, Chey said.

articles.title: TMC Crisis Deepens: 60 MLAs Skip Mamata Meeting, Two Expelled Amid Signature Forgery Probe
articles.description: Following a bruising electoral defeat in West Bengal, Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress faces massive internal turmoil. A crucial strategy meeting at Kalighat was cancelled after 60 TMC MLAs skipped it, prompting Mamata to allege a BJP conspiracy. Adding to the crisis, the CID is investigating a signature forgery case involving a party letter to the Assembly secretariat, summoning MP Abhishek Banerjee. The TMC quickly expelled MLAs Ritabrata Banerjee and Sandipan Saha, who flagged the fraud. With over 120 municipal councillors resigning and Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar attending Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari’s meeting, Mamata's grip on the party appears under threat.

articles.title: Punjab Set for Assembly Elections Next Year
articles.description: Punjab Set for Assembly Elections Next Year

articles.title: Mamata Banerjee Announces Protest Despite Police Denial, Alleges BJP Plot Against TMC
articles.description: Mamata Banerjee has announced that the Trinamool Congress will proceed with its planned protest in Kolkata despite police denying permission. The TMC chief said she was prepared to be arrested and accused the BJP of attempting to break her party through defections, police pressure and misuse of institutions.Banerjee also defended Abhishek Banerjee amid growing political tensions and claimed that democratic protests were being obstructed. Her remarks come as the TMC faces internal challenges and reports of leaders moving closer to the BJP.

articles.title: Rapper Badshah’s Sago nightclub in Chandigarh sealed over building rule violations
articles.description: Chandigarh Deputy Commissioner Nishant Yadav said Sago by Badshah failed to rectify violations despite being granted considerable time and multiple opportunities.

articles.title: Suvendu government’s crackdown on illegal immigration is a strategic shock for Jamaat
articles.description: The BJP’s decisive win in West Bengal, coupled with immediate and decisive policy actions, threatens the operational space that Jamaat-e-Islami has cultivated over years through its network of madrasas and control over border constituencies

articles.title: Kerala Assembly: LDF walks out, accuses UDF of neglecting fuel crisis
articles.description: LDF accuses UDF of neglecting fuel crisis in Assembly walkout, highlighting the impact on ordinary households amid rising prices.

articles.title: Travelling to Vietnam post July 1? Indians will now need this additional document to enter the nation
articles.description: The rule could affect tourists, business travellers, students, expatriates, and passengers using Vietnam as a Southeast Asian transit hub

articles.title: Arava Sreedhar resigns as Government Whip; resignation accepted with immediate effect
articles.description: The government accepts Railway Koduru MLA Arava Sreedhar's resignation as government whip. The Jana Sena legislator had faced allegations from a woman employee.

articles.title: From Bar To The Bench: Story of Justice Mohana, The New Top Court Judge
articles.description: Justice VS Mohana is only the second woman advocate to be appointed directly from the Bar to the apex court

articles.title: Telangana Formation Day: Governor calls it symbol of martyrs' victory
articles.description: Governor Shiv Pratap Shukla greeted people on Telangana Formation Day, calling it a symbol of martyrs' sacrifice. He highlighted the state's global identity in IT and pharma. CM A Revanth Reddy also paid tributes to martyrs.

articles.title: Karnataka cabinet formation: Siddaramaiah, leaders huddle in Delhi
articles.description: Karnataka caretaker CM Siddaramaiah and other state leaders are in Delhi to finalize the new cabinet with the party high command. The meetings come ahead of CM-designate DK Shivakumar's swearing-in ceremony, amid intense lobbying for ministerial berths.

articles.title: Democracy Not For Defending Forces Undermining India: Kiren Rijiju
articles.description: Union Minister Kiren Rijiju slammed those defending 'anti-India gangs', saying democratic freedoms shouldn't undermine national security. Separately, CJP's Abhijeet Dipke will lead a protest over NEET-UG irregularities amid a CBI probe.

articles.title: BJP's Poonawalla slams Karnataka govt for 'power games', not governance
articles.description: BJP's Shehzad Poonawalla attacked the Karnataka government, alleging it prioritizes 'power games' over governance. He claimed key civic and infrastructure issues are being ignored while leaders are busy with cabinet formation and political negotiations.

articles.title: TMC attacks: Congress MP Pramod Tiwari slams BJP for 'hooliganism'
articles.description: Congress MP Pramod Tiwari has slammed the BJP for 'hooliganism' over alleged attacks on TMC leaders Abhishek and Kalyan Banerjee, questioning police inaction. The attack comes as Mamata Banerjee is set to lead a protest in Kolkata.

articles.title: The Economics of War: Why money matters in the US-Iran conflict
articles.description: Despite overwhelming military superiority, the US has to deal with rising costs and logistical constraints in Iran. Money does matter but history teaches us it's not everything

This news details a recent gathering hosted at Bhai Sawinder Singh Ji’s Maryland [...] The post Pakistan Punjab Minister Meets with Sikh Leaderhip in Maryland USA appeared first on Sanjhi Soch Worldwide Newspaper .

articles.title: Bail is rule, jail an exception, but not absolute: Supreme Court
articles.description: Bail rules: The Supreme Court said bail remains the rule and jail the exception, but courts must weigh facts, evidence, offence severity and law. The ruling came in a Jammu murder case, granting relief to two elderly accused while denying others.

articles.title: No Women, Few Minorities: How Hegseth Reshaped The Navy's Future Leadership
articles.description: The revised slate of 22 nominees for one-star admiral, released publicly in late May, differs significantly from the composition of the broader Navy force.

articles.title: Mamata Banerjee protest LIVE: Ex-Bengal CM says will go ahead with mega Kolkata sit-in despite no permit
articles.description: Mamata Banerjee protest LIVE: TMC supremo Mamata Banerjee has planned a protest today at Kolkata's Rani Rashmoni Avenue over attacks on party leaders, including her nephew Abhishek Banerjee, and Railways' hawker eviction drive.

articles.title: Temporary workers eligible for pension, Supreme Court rules
articles.description: The Supreme Court has ruled that long-serving temporary employees cannot be denied pension benefits and ordered the Centre to release dues within three months.

articles.title: Hindi and Marathi share natural closeness, cultural unity: Maha CM Devendra Fadnavis
articles.description: Mother tongue education: Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis says Hindi and Marathi share cultural unity. He backs mother-tongue learning under NEP, saying native-language education improves learning outcomes while respecting all Indian languages and heritage.

articles.title: 'Unnecessary and burdensome': Shashi Tharoor questions full rendition of Vande Mataram at official events
articles.description: Tharoor argued that the portion of Vande Mataram traditionally rendered in public was roughly the same length as the national anthem and had long been widely accepted and respected.

articles.title: 'You'd be in prison if not for me': Trump blasts Netanyahu over Israeli strikes on Lebanon, tells Bibi 'everyone hates him'
articles.description: US President Donald Trump lambasted Israel Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu during a heated phone call on Monday over Israeli strikes on Lebanon.

articles.title: Sankashti Chaturthi 2026 Tomorrow: This Special Fast Comes Once Every 3 Years, Avoid These 5 Mistakes
articles.description: Sankashti Chaturthi 2026 will be observed on 3 June. Dedicated to Lord Ganesha, this rare fast holds special significance. Devotees should follow key traditions and avoid these five common mistakes.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by Javier Marías: ‘We lose everything because everything remains except us, and therefore any form of posterity may be...’ Spanish author’s golden words on fragility of human existence and persistence of memory
articles.description: Javier Marias, a renowned Spanish writer, is remembered for his profound novels. His work delved into memory, betrayal, and time's passage. Marias' elegant prose and philosophical depth earned him global acclaim. He authored fifteen novels and numerous essays. His books were translated into many languages. Marias' legacy continues to inspire readers worldwide.

articles.title: Lalit Modi Claims He Was Sushmita Sen's 'Kept Boyfriend,' Calls Himself 'Diamond Digger'
articles.description: Lalit Modi has defended ex-girlfriend Sushmita Sen against past 'gold digger' accusations, claiming he was the 'kept boyfriend' in the relationship as she paid for everything. Calling himself a diamond digger, Modi praised Sushmita's independence.

articles.title: Netanyahu says told Trump Israel to continue southern Lebanon ops as Hezbollah 'accepts' US-backed mutual halt proposal
articles.description: Amid rising tensions, Netanyahu had ordered strikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs as Iranian state TV warned a US-Iran ceasefire may collapse if Israeli operations in Lebanon continue.

articles.description: Lebanon's US embassy said that Hezbollah had accepted a US proposal for a "mutual cessation of attacks".

articles.title: Scott Pelley Blasts CBS News Leadership In Heated 60 Minutes Staff Meeting
articles.description: Scott Pelley, a correspondent for 60 Minutes, voiced strong discontent with CBS News leadership in a recent staff meeting, criticizing the firings of senior staff, including executive producer Tanya Simon.

articles.title: Himachal kicks off Census 2027; snow-bound regions get early start
articles.description: Himachal Pradesh launched the self-enumeration phase of Census 2027, with Governor Kavinder Gupta urging citizens to participate via an online portal. Snow-bound and tribal regions of the state will be covered early, from September 11 to September 30.

articles.title: Forgery row: Ex-TMC MLA Sandipan Saha writes to WB Assembly Speaker
articles.description: Expelled TMC MLA Sandipan Saha alleges his signature was fabricated on a resolution and has written to the WB Assembly Speaker demanding a probe. Suvendu Adhikari has attacked the TMC over the case, warning that forgers will not be spared.

articles.title: Eknath Shinde sidesteps Sena reunion talk, says party has people’s mandate
articles.description: Responding to questions on remarks by Sena (UBT) leader Ambadas Danve and Shiv Sena minister Abdul Sattar on the possibility of the two factions coming together, Shinde avoided a direct reply.

articles.title: TMC expels Sandipan Saha, Ritabrata Banerjee for 'anti-party' acts
articles.description: TMC expelled leaders Sandipan Saha and Ritabrata Banerjee for 'anti-party' activities. Saha retorted he was pleased to be ousted for upholding morality. The expulsion follows their complaint alleging forged signatures on a party document.

articles.title: Ranveer Singh Gets Legal Support As Veteran Producer TP Aggarwal Moves Court Against FWICE Directive
articles.description: TP Aggarwal has challenged FWICE’s non-cooperation directive against Ranveer Singh in court amid the Don 3 exit controversy.

articles.title: 'Cooker Only' Audio Row Deepens Congress Rift As Zameer Denies Viral Recording Claims | Watch
articles.description: A purported audio conversation involving Karnataka minister Zameer Ahmed Khan and Congress leader Mohammed Siraj has sparked fresh controversy over alleged internal sabotage during the Davanagere South bypoll. The viral clip, whose authenticity remains unverified, includes references to the SDPI's "cooker" symbol and has intensified political speculation ahead of Karnataka's new Cabinet formation. Zameer and Siraj have dismissed the recording as fake and AI-generated. The row has revived tensions within Congress over candidate selection and bypoll performance.

articles.description: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay defended his choice of wearing a suit at a Tiruchirappalli rally, saying attire should not be reserved for those in power.

articles.title: ‘Fairfax, a magnet for VIOLENT ILLEGAL ALIENS!’: Tiffany TARGETS sanctuary policies at House hearing
articles.description: A heated debate over immigration and public safety unfolded during a House Judiciary Committee hearing as Congressman Tom Tiffany questioned former Virginia Attorney General Jason Miyares about the impact of sanctuary policies. Tiffany raised concerns that sanctuary-style policies may be attracting dangerous criminal organizations, including MS-13 and Tren de Aragua (TdA), and asked whether such policies are turning communities into magnets for violent illegal aliens.

articles.title: Government can take back its land when needed, says Urban Affairs Minister on Gymkhana issue
articles.description: The Delhi Gymkhana Club has been asked by Union Urban Affairs Ministry on May 22 to hand over its premises by June 5 as the 27.3-acre plot is required for “strengthening and securing defence infrastructure”
